foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Gallstones are the causative factor in >90% of cholecystitis cases; understanding stone pathogenesis is essential

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Knowledge of hepatic arterial supply via cystic artery and Calot's triangle anatomy is critical for safe cholecystectomy

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Cholecystitis can coexist with CBD stones causing obstructive jaundice; distinguishing biliary obstruction guides management

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Acute cholecystitis is the most common emergency presentation requiring urgent cholecystectomy

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Concomitant CBD stones occur in 10-15% of cholecystitis patients, altering surgical planning

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Ascending cholangitis is a life-threatening complication when cholecystitis is complicated by biliary obstruction

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Chronic cholecystitis is a risk factor for gallbladder carcinoma; incidental cancer found in 1-2% of cholecystectomy specimens

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Acalculous biliary dyskinesia mimics cholecystitis and is a key differential in stone-negative biliary colic

AN47.6: Explain the anatomical basis of Splenic notch, Accessory spleens, Kehr’s
AN47.7: Mention the clinical importance of Calot’s triangle
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Cholecystitis across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
